title: Method of maintaining soil strength and stability
abstract: A method of maintaining soil strength and stability involves positioning a sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material (12, 12A) on a selected area where the strengthening and stabilization of the soil is desired. The sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material (12, 12A) includes a fabric layer (14, 14A) that forms a base of the soil stabilization and strengthening material. The lower surface of the fabric layer (14, 14A) is positioned against and engages the selected area. A moisture impervious layer (16, 16A, 16B) is coupled to the upper surface of the fabric layer (14, 14A). A particulate aggregate material (18, 18A, 18B) is embedded in the moisture impervious layer (16, 16A, 16B).
claims: Claims CLAIMS I claim: 1. A method of maintaining soil strength and stability comprising: positioning a sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material on a selected area where stability and strength of soil is desired, the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material comprising: a fabric layer that forms a base of the soil stabilization and strengthening material, the lower surface of the fabric layer being positioned against and engaging the selected area; a moisture impervious layer coupled to the upper surface of the fabric layer; and a particulate aggregate material embedded in the moisture impervious layer. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein: the fabric layer is a geotextile. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein: the fabric layer is permeable to moisture. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein: the fabric layer is at least one of woven, non-woven, needle punched, and heat bonded fabric material. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein: the fabric material is a polymeric fiber material is at least one of polypropylene, polyethylene, polyester, polyamide, and polyurea. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein: the moisture impervious layer is a rubber and/or elastomeric material. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein: the rubber and/or elastomeric material is formed from at least one of polymers and copolymers of urethane, silicone, acrylate, vinyl chloride, acrylonitrile, ethylene, propylene, butadiene, isoprene, styrene, vinyl acetate, methacrylate, methyl methacrylate, vinyl acetate, and combinations of these. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein: the particulate aggregate material has an average particle size of from 10 mm or less. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein: the particulate aggregate material has an average particle size of from 6 mm or less. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein: the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material further comprises a second moisture impervious layer positioned over and coupled to the upper surface the moisture impervious layer having the particulate aggregate material embedded therein; and a second particulate aggregate material embedded in the second moisture impervious layer. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein: the fabric layer has a thickness of from 0.5 mm to 7.0 mm, a weight of from 3 oz/yd 2 (102 g/m 2 ) to 50 oz/yd 2 (1,695 g/m 2 ) as measured according to ASTM D5261, and a grab tensile strength of from 50 lbs (0.222 kN) to 1000 lbs (4.44 kN), as measured according to ASTM D4632. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein: the moisture impervious layer has a thickness of from 0.5 mm to 7.0 mm and a weight of from 3 oz/yd 2 (102 g/m 2 ) to 250 oz/yd 2 (8,476 g/m 2 ). 13. The method of claim 1, wherein: the aggregate covers from 5% to 99% of the surface of the moisture impervious layer. 14. The method of claim 1, wherein: the weight of the particulate aggregate material of the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material is from 5 oz/yd 2 (170 g/m 2 ) to 500 oz/yd 2 (16,953 g/m 2 ). 15. The method of claim 10, wherein: the particulate aggregate material embedded in the moisture impervious layer has an average particle size that is from >1 to 5 times larger than the average particle size of the second particulate aggregate material. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein: the particulate aggregate material embedded in the moisture impervious layer has an average particle size of from 2 mm to 4 mm and the average particle size of the second particulate aggregate material is from 1 mm to 2 mm. 14 17. The method of claim 1, wherein: the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material has a width of from 3 feet to 20 feet (0.3 m to 6.1 m) and a length of from 10 feet to 300 feet (3.0 m to 91.4 m). 18. A method of maintaining soil strength and stability comprising: positioning a sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material on a selected area where stability and strength of soil is desired, the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material having a width of from 3 feet to 20 feet (0.3 m to 6.1 m) and a length of from 10 feet to 300 feet (3.0 m to 91.4 m), the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material comprising: a geotextile fabric layer that is at least one of a woven, non-woven, needle punched, and heat bonded fabric material and is permeable to moisture and forms a base of the soil stabilization and strengthening material, the lower surface of the fabric layer being positioned against and engaging the selected area, the fabric layer having a thickness of from 0.5 mm to 7.0 mm, a weight of from 3 oz/yd 2 (102 g/m 2 ) to 50 oz/yd 2 (1,695 g/m 2 ), as measured according to ASTM D5261, and a grab tensile strength of from 50 lbs (0.222 kN) to 1000 lbs (4.44 kN), as measured according to ASTM D4632; a moisture impervious layer that is a rubber and/or elastomeric material coupled to the upper surface of the fabric layer, the moisture impervious layer having a thickness of from 0.5 mm to 7.0 mm and a weight of from 3 oz/yd 2 (102 g/m 2 ) to 250 oz/yd 2 (8,476 g/m 2 ); and a particulate aggregate material embedded in the moisture impervious layer, the particulate aggregate material having an average particle size of from 10 mm or less and covering from 5% to 99% of the surface of the moisture impervious layer, the weight of the particulate aggregate material of the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material being from 5 oz/yd 2 (170 g/m 2 ) to 500 oz/yd 2 (16,953 g/m 2 ). 15 19. The method of claim 18, wherein: the sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material further comprises a second moisture impervious layer positioned over and coupled to the upper surface the moisture impervious layer having the particulate aggregate material embedded therein; and a second particulate aggregate material embedded in the second moisture impervious layer; and wherein the particulate aggregate material embedded in the moisture impervious layer has an average particle size that is from >1 to 5 times larger than the average particle size of the second particulate aggregate material. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein: the particulate aggregate material embedded in the moisture impervious layer has an average particle size of from 2 mm to 4 mm and the average particle size of the second particulate aggregate material is from 1 mm to 2 mm. 21. A method of forming a sheet of soil stabilization and strengthening material, the method comprising: applying an uncured moisture impervious material to an upper surface of a fabric layer; applying a particulate aggregate material to the uncured moisture impervious rubber and/or elastomeric material so that the particulate aggregate material is embedded in the moisture impervious rubber and/or elastomeric material; and allowing the moisture impervious material to cure so that the embedded particulate aggregate material is coupled and retained therein. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein: the fabric layer is a geotextile. 16 23. The method of claim 21, wherein: the fabric layer is permeable to moisture. 24. The method of claim 21, wherein: the fabric layer is at least one of woven, non-woven, needle punched, and heat bonded fabric material. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein: the polymeric fiber material is at least one of polypropylene, polyethylene, polyester, polyamide, and polyurea. 26. The method of claim 21, wherein: the moisture impervious layer is a rubber and/or elastomeric material. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein: the rubber and/or elastomeric material is formed from at least one of polymers and copolymers of urethane, silicone, acrylate, vinyl chloride, acrylonitrile, ethylene, propylene, butadiene, isoprene, styrene, vinyl acetate, methacrylate, methyl methacrylate, vinyl acetate, and combinations of these. 28. The method of claim 21, wherein: the particulate aggregate material has an average particle size of from 10 mm or less. 29. The method of claim 21, wherein: the particulate aggregate material has an average particle size of from 6 mm or less. 17 30. A soil stabilization and strengthening material that can be applied to a selected area where prevention of soil erosion is desired, the soil stabilization and strengthening material comprising: a fabric layer that forms a base of the soil stabilization and strengthening material, the lower surface of the fabric layer being positioned against and engaging the selected area; a moisture impervious layer coupled to the upper surface of the fabric layer; and a particulate aggregate material embedded in the moisture impervious layer. 18
